description: §17 Worked Example 3 shape (valid) — an `else-if=` properly anchored to a preceding `if=` sibling, chain terminating at `else`, does NOT fire E-CTRL-002. Conditions use the atomic `@step.eq(n)` call form, legal bare per §5.2. W-PROGRAM-SPA-INFERRED co-fires incidentally at severity info; not asserted.
rationale: §17.1.1: `else-if=` SHALL be immediately preceded at the same parent level by an element carrying `if=` or `else-if=`. Here the `else-if=` directly follows the chain-opening `if=` element, satisfying the precondition, so E-CTRL-002 SHALL stay silent. Per §17.1.1 the chain opens with `if=`, continues with `else-if=`, and terminates with `else`.
